name: Alien LT Trace-to-3D
summary: Convert manual or SVG tonearm traces into a parameterized three-dimensional wand model in the browser.
description: A browser-based engineering tool for tracing tonearm-wand outlines, refining explicit geometry parameters, previewing an OpenCascade model, and exporting gated STL and STEP files.
capabilities: Create and edit multiple manual or SVG-based trace documents.
Generate a parameterized OpenCascade tonearm-wand model from approved trace and parameter state.
Preview the generated geometry in an interactive Three.js viewport.
Export STL and STEP geometry after built-in validation gates pass.
Save and restore a workspace containing trace state and three-dimensional parameters.

inputs.name: trace_geometry
inputs.type: manual trace, SVG, or Engrove workspace data
inputs.description: Two-dimensional outlines, reference geometry, named zones, and measurement data used to define the wand body.

inputs.name: wand_parameters
inputs.description: Explicit dimensions and shape controls used by the OpenCascade geometry engine.

outputs.name: stl_geometry
outputs.description: Triangulated geometry emitted only after the watertight export gate passes.

outputs.name: step_geometry
outputs.description: Boundary-representation geometry for downstream CAD work.

outputs.name: workspace
outputs.description: Persisted multi-trace and three-dimensional parameter state for continued work.

whenToUse: Use it to translate explicit top or side trace geometry into a repeatable parametric wand model.
Use it to iterate trace metadata, measurements, and model parameters before exporting CAD geometry.
Use it when a browser-based local workflow is preferable to installing a desktop CAD utility.
whenNotToUse: Do not use it as a substitute for certified dimensional inspection or structural certification.
Do not use it to model the separated linear-tracking mechanism fields excluded by the repository contract.
Do not treat an exported file as automatically manufacturable without independent engineering review.
limitations: OpenCascade WASM is loaded from a version-pinned external CDN asset and therefore requires network access on first load.
Geometry quality depends on the supplied trace, dimensions, parameter choices, and successful validation gates.
The tool provides engineering assistance but no certification, warranty, or guaranteed manufacturing fitness.

mayClaim: The application runs client-side after its static assets and version-pinned OpenCascade WASM have loaded.
The application generates parameterized tonearm-wand geometry from validated trace and parameter inputs.
STL and STEP export remain subject to built-in geometry validation gates.
negativeClaimBoundaries: No public programmatic backend API is provided.
No user account, registration, or OAuth flow is required.
No certification or laboratory-grade accuracy claim is made.
mustNotClaim: Do not claim scientifically proven results.

faq.question: Does the tool upload project data to a server?
faq.answer: No. Application data is processed in the browser; the site serves static application assets and a version-pinned external OpenCascade WASM file.

faq.question: What files can the tool export?
faq.answer: It can export gated STL and STEP geometry, plus project data used to continue the trace-to-3D workflow.

faq.question: Is the generated geometry certified for manufacturing?
faq.answer: No. The tool provides engineering assistance and validation gates, not certification or guaranteed manufacturing fitness.
